checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=134820651.51 (2012-12-31); ReceivablesNetCurrent=153342341.2 (2013-12-31); ReceivablesNetCurrent=156708905.92 (2014-12-31); ReceivablesNetCurrent=147680430.49 (2015-09-30) — no Revenues/SalesRevenueNet concept was provided in the dataset for comparison
evidence.location: ReceivablesNetCurrent series; Revenues concept absent from provided data

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Only aggregate Assets=8249955402.5 (2012-12-31) through Assets=8184577557.51 (2015-09-30) is provided; no IntangibleAssets, Goodwill, OtherAssets or CapitalizedCosts line items were supplied
evidence.location: Assets series; no soft-asset breakdown present

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-138058360.73 (FY2012) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=230949432.26 (FY2012); NetIncomeLoss=-102124856.72 (FY2013) vs OCF=285191584.86 (FY2013); NetIncomeLoss=114123880.96 (FY2014) vs OCF=411658963.77 (FY2014)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet, CostOfGoodsSold, or Revenues concepts were provided in the dataset
evidence.location: Data coverage gap - no inventory/COGS/revenue series supplied

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: ProfitLoss=-21859047.17 (Q1 2013), -48831939.64 (Q2 2013), -21093645.49 (Q3 2013) showing volatility, followed by ProfitLoss=35856190.7 (Q1 2014), 26886129.77 (Q2 2014), 29090417.87 (Q3 2014) which is comparatively smooth, then a step-up to ProfitLoss=26746965.83 (Q1 2015), 47390476.1 (Q2 2015), 47091531.34 (Q3 2015) that plateaus at a higher level for two consecutive quarters

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=6276042896.96 (2012-12-31) declining to Liabilities=5666150912.6 (2015-09-30), while Assets=8249955402.5 (2012-12-31) similarly declined/flat to Assets=8184577557.51 (2015-09-30) — liabilities declined roughly in proportion to a non-growing asset base, consistent with deleveraging rather than concealment; no allowance/reserve line items were provided to assess separately

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Filing chronology shows S-11 (2013-07-18) through multiple S-11/A amendments (2013-08-23, 2013-09-23, 2013-10-04, 2013-10-17) culminating in EFFECT (2013-10-29) and 424B4 (2013-10-31), consistent with a routine REIT IPO registration process; a second S-11 shelf process occurred in 2014 (S-11 filed 2014-05-27 through 424B4 2014-06-26). No NT 10-K/NT 10-Q or 10-K/A restatement filings appear in the chronology

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Dataset provides Assets, Liabilities, StockholdersEquity, Cash, Receivables, Debt, D&A, G&A, Interest Expense, and cash flow statement totals for 2012-2015, but omits Revenues, CostOfGoodsSold, InventoryNet, IntangibleAssets/Goodwill, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, and any segment-level detail, limiting several checklist assessments (CL1, CL2, CL4, part of CL6) to insufficient_data

top_signals: No revenue/COGS/inventory data provided, preventing assessment of receivables-vs-revenue and margin trends (CL1, CL4 insufficient_data)

Persistent gap between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow (NetIncomeLoss=-138058360.73 vs OCF=230949432.26 for FY2012; NetIncomeLoss=114123880.96 vs OCF=411658963.77 for FY2014) is largely explained by heavy D&A typical of a REIT (DepreciationAndAmortization=379376365.62 FY2014), so not strong standalone fraud evidence

Quarterly net income shows a smoothing/step-change pattern (ProfitLoss steady near 27-36M through 2014, then plateaus near 47M in Q2-Q3 2015) that warrants monitoring but is not conclusive given limited context

No NT filings, restatements, or 10-K/A observed in filing chronology; amendment activity (S-11/A, 424B, 8-K/A, 4/A) appears consistent with routine IPO/registration and equity-offering activity for a newly public REIT (S-11 filed 2013-07-18, IPO effective 2013-10-29)

Liabilities and debt balances declined modestly in tandem with a roughly flat/slightly shrinking asset base (Assets=8249955402.5 at 2012-12-31 vs Assets=8184577557.51 at 2015-09-30; Liabilities=6276042896.96 at 2012-12-31 vs Liabilities=5666150912.6 at 2015-09-30), consistent with deleveraging rather than liability understatement
